name: Temporal Binning via Grouped Z-Projection
description:
Time-domain astronomy often involves high-cadence imaging where individual frames suffer from low signal-to-noise ratios (SNR). Your task is to perform temporal binning on a WASP-12 exoplanet transit time-series (186 calibrated FITS frames in `~/AstroImages/time_series/raw/`) to create a time-binned data cube and mathematically verify the noise reduction. 

Perform a temporal binning by grouping every 10 frames using a Median projection. Save the resulting binned stack to `~/AstroImages/time_series/binned/wasp12_binned_10x.tif`. To verify the noise reduction, measure the Standard Deviation of the pixel values within a specific background Region of Interest (ROI) at X=200, Y=200, Width=400, Height=400 for Slice 1 of the original unbinned stack and Slice 1 of the new binned stack. 

Create a noise report at `~/AstroImages/time_series/binned/noise_report.txt` containing the two standard deviation values and the computed reduction factor (Raw / Binned) using exactly this format:

Raw_StdDev: <value>
Binned_StdDev: <value>
Reduction_Factor: <value>
